petitioner_background: The petitioner is an attorney specializing in claims management, currently serving as the managing attorney of a firm providing guidance on personal injury law and navigating the claims management system to United Kingdom nationals in California. He sought classification as an individual of extraordinary ability in business under INA § 203(b)(1)(A). He has worked as an attorney within the claims management industry since 1999, beginning as a trainee solicitor and then assistant solicitor handling personal injury matters on both claimant and defendant insurance sides. He founded his first firm in 2003, founded another organization in 2009 where he served as chairman, and founded his current firm in 2016.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted published articles in professional publications about him relating to his work in claims management. Specifically, the record contains a Claims Management Magazine article discussing the petitioner's formation of his firm and including an interview with him regarding the future of claims management companies. Information about the magazine indicated that it is a publication for professionals in the claims management field. Additional articles included a Modern Claims Magazine article addressing industry issues, a Modern Law Magazine feature where the petitioner chaired a roundtable discussion, and several other roundtable discussions, question-and-answer articles, and opinion editorials in various professional publications featuring him as an expert.
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO found that the record contains published articles in professional publications that are about the petitioner relating to his work in claims management. The Claims Management Magazine article discussed the petitioner's formation of his firm and included an interview with him regarding the future of claims management companies, and information in the record indicated the magazine is a publication for professionals in the claims management field. Accordingly, the AAO determined the petitioner demonstrated that he meets the published material criterion, reversing the Director's finding on this criterion.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: Evidence that the petitioner participated as a judge of the work of others at several claims management conferences, including multiple years of an annual event. The petitioner served on multiple judging panels for claims industry conferences and awards since 2010. In 2012, he served as the chair of the judging panel for an industry awards event. He also judged the 2013 and 2015 industry events, including an event 'designed to celebrate the successes and talents of those in the claims industry.'
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO agreed with the Director that the petitioner met the judging criterion, finding that the record contains evidence that the petitioner has participated as a judge of the work of others at several claims management conferences, including multiple years of an annual event.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: Evidence demonstrating that the petitioner, as the founder of his firm, served in a leading role for an organization having a distinguished reputation. The petitioner founded his first firm in 2003 and served as its managing attorney, founded another organization in 2009 where he served as chairman, and founded his current firm in 2016 serving as managing attorney. Under his leadership, his firm received industry awards including an award at a 2012 event and another award at a 2015 event.
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO agreed with the Director that the petitioner met the leading or critical role criterion, finding that the petitioner, as the founder of his firm, served in a leading role for an organization having a distinguished reputation.

outcome_reasoning: Upon de novo review, the AAO sustained the appeal, finding the petitioner met at least three of the evidentiary criteria (published material, judging, and leading or critical role) and demonstrated sustained national or international acclaim through the totality of evidence. In the final merits determination, the AAO emphasized the petitioner's frequent authorship and interviews in professional publications, his frequent invitations to speak at professional events based on his claims management expertise, his 2011 invitation to participate in a United Kingdom Ministry of Justice working group focused on reviewing claims management regulation (and subsequent permanent invitation to join), his service on multiple judging panels including as chair of an industry awards panel, and industry awards received under his leadership. The AAO concluded this evidence demonstrated a 'career of acclaimed work in the field' and placed him as one of the small percentage at the top of his field, establishing extraordinary ability in business with sustained international acclaim.
legal_citations: Rijal v. USCIS, 772 F. Supp. 2d 1339 (W.D. Wash. 2011)
Matter of Price, 20 I&N Dec. 953 (Assoc. Comm'r 1994)
Matter of Skirball Cultural Ctr., 25 I&N Dec. 799 (AAO 2012)
